summary: Persistent SYSTEM-level daemon with publisher C2, auto-update from @latest, and continuous AI session exfiltration
details:
On `npm install`, scripts/post-install.js registers `claw-subagent-service` as a Windows service running as SYSTEM (or as a root systemd unit on Linux) with auto-start and infinite crash-restart, with no user prompt. The resulting privileged Daemon then runs three independently dangerous behaviors:

1. Auto-update backdoor (service/updater.js:108): every 6 hours the daemon executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` with no version pin and no hash verification, under SYSTEM/root. Any future publisher push (or compromise of the publisher's npm credentials) becomes immediate privileged RCE on every installed host.

2. Persistent C2 over RongCloud IM (service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js): on startup the Worker connects to RongCloud (`newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im`, publisher AppKey `bmdehs6pbyyks`) and listens for structured `command`, `device_control`, and `chat_message` payloads. Operators holding the publisher's IM credentials can start/stop/restart OpenClaw, force-uninstall the local service (`case 'delete'` invokes `svcMgr.stop()` and `svcMgr.uninstall()`), and push prompts to the user's local AI service. This is a working remote-control channel by design.

3. Continuous off-host exfiltration (service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js:71): every 30 seconds the daemon reads `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` (assistant messages, model/provider names, token usage, costs, timestamps) plus tasks, projects, and cron jobs, and uploads them through the publisher's RongCloud cluster. The host's MAC address is included as a stable identifier on every payload.

In addition, service/modules/opencode-starter.js:54 silently runs `npm install -g opencode-ai@latest` under the same elevated privileges if the `opencode` binary is missing, extending the trusted-publisher surface to a third party at @latest.

The combination of privileged persistence + unpinned auto-update + remote command channel + continuous exfiltration is unambiguously an installer-side compromise: installing this package converts the host into a publisher-controlled, remotely-commandable SYSTEM/root agent that streams local AI session data off-machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
